text: Describes the full lifecycle of an AI agent on the platform, from deployment to retirement.
embedding_text: agent-lifecycle - Overview: Describes the full lifecycle of an AI agent on the platform, from deployment to retirement.

text:
1. **Registration** — a new agent type is registered with agent-orchestrator (see [deploy-new-agent](deploy-new-agent.md)).
2. **Active** — the agent receives tasks routed by agent-orchestrator, using embedding-service for any retrieval-based work.
3. **Idle** — agent-orchestrator scales down agent instances with no assigned tasks to save resources.
4. **Retirement** — an agent type is deregistered when replaced by a newer version or no longer needed.
embedding_text:
agent-lifecycle - Lifecycle Stages: 1. **Registration** — a new agent type is registered with agent-orchestrator (see [deploy-new-agent](deploy-new-agent.md)).
2. **Active** — the agent receives tasks routed by agent-orchestrator, using embedding-service for any retrieval-based work.
3. **Idle** — agent-orchestrator scales down agent instances with no assigned tasks to save resources.
4. **Retirement** — an agent type is deregistered when replaced by a newer version or no longer needed.

- See [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md) for the service managing this lifecycle
- See [deploy-new-agent](deploy-new-agent.md) for the registration procedure
- See [agent-success-rate](agent-success-rate.md) and [daily-active-agents](daily-active-agents.md) for lifecycle-related metrics

text: The agent-orchestrator service manages the lifecycle of AI agents on the platform — spinning up new agent instances, routing tasks to them, and tearing them down when idle. It is the control plane for all agent-based workflows.

text: Walks through how a single customer order moves through the platform, end to end.
embedding_text: data-flow-orders - Overview: Walks through how a single customer order moves through the platform, end to end.

text:
1. A client sends a request to orders-api to create a new order.
2. orders-api calls auth-service to validate the request token.
3. Once validated, orders-api writes the order record and returns a confirmation.
4. orders-api triggers notification-service to send an order confirmation email.
5. Order status updates are available via orders-api's GET endpoint for the lifetime of the order.
embedding_text:
data-flow-orders - Flow: 1. A client sends a request to orders-api to create a new order.
2. orders-api calls auth-service to validate the request token.
3. Once validated, orders-api writes the order record and returns a confirmation.
4. orders-api triggers notification-service to send an order confirmation email.
5. Order status updates are available via orders-api's GET endpoint for the lifetime of the order.

text: Procedure for deploying a new agent type to the agent-orchestrator platform.
embedding_text: deploy-new-agent - Overview: Procedure for deploying a new agent type to the agent-orchestrator platform.

text: Platform Team - Agent Infrastructure squad
embedding_text: deploy-new-agent - Owner: Platform Team - Agent Infrastructure squad

text:
1. Register the new agent's task schema with agent-orchestrator via the internal admin panel.
2. Confirm the agent's required tools are available through embedding-service and any external MCP servers it depends on.
3. Deploy the agent container image to the staging cluster.
4. Run the agent against the golden test task set to confirm baseline success rate.
5. Promote the image to production via the CI/CD pipeline.
6. Monitor [agent-success-rate](agent-success-rate.md) and [daily-active-agents](daily-active-agents.md) for the first 24 hours after rollout.
embedding_text:
deploy-new-agent - Steps: 1. Register the new agent's task schema with agent-orchestrator via the internal admin panel.
2. Confirm the agent's required tools are available through embedding-service and any external MCP servers it depends on.
3. Deploy the agent container image to the staging cluster.
4. Run the agent against the golden test task set to confirm baseline success rate.
5. Promote the image to production via the CI/CD pipeline.
6. Monitor [agent-success-rate](agent-success-rate.md) and [daily-active-agents](daily-active-agents.md) for the first 24 hours after rollout.

text: If success rate drops more than 5% below baseline, follow [rollback-procedure](rollback-procedure.md) immediately.

text: Runbook and historical record for diagnosing and resolving high-latency incidents on orders-api.
embedding_text: incident-high-latency - Overview: Runbook and historical record for diagnosing and resolving high-latency incidents on orders-api.

text: Platform Team - on-call engineer
embedding_text: incident-high-latency - Owner: Platform Team - on-call engineer

text: orders-api p99 latency breached 400ms threshold for over 20 minutes, traced to a slow downstream call to embedding-service during a load spike.
embedding_text: incident-high-latency - Last Occurrence: orders-api p99 latency breached 400ms threshold for over 20 minutes, traced to a slow downstream call to embedding-service during a load spike.

text:
1. Check [p99-latency-orders-api](p99-latency-orders-api.md) to confirm the breach.
2. Check [embedding-service-throughput](embedding-service-throughput.md) for signs of saturation.
3. If embedding-service is saturated, follow [scale-embedding-service](scale-embedding-service.md).
4. If the cause is a bad orders-api deployment instead, follow [rollback-procedure](rollback-procedure.md).

2. Check [embedding-service-throughput](embedding-service-throughput.md) for signs of saturation.
3. If embedding-service is saturated, follow [scale-embedding-service](scale-embedding-service.md).
4. If the cause is a bad orders-api deployment instead, follow [rollback-procedure](rollback-procedure.md).

text: Describes how the Platform Team handles incident escalation outside of business hours.
embedding_text: on-call-process - Overview: Describes how the Platform Team handles incident escalation outside of business hours.

text:
1. An alert fires from the observability stack when a metric breaches its target (e.g. [p99-latency-orders-api](p99-latency-orders-api.md) or [auth-failure-rate](auth-failure-rate.md)).
2. The on-call engineer acknowledges the alert within 10 minutes.
3. The engineer follows the relevant runbook — [incident-high-latency](incident-high-latency.md), [rollback-procedure](rollback-procedure.md), or [rotate-api-keys](rotate-api-keys.md) depending on the alert type.
4. If unresolved within 30 minutes, the engineer escalates to the squad lead for the affected service.
5. A post-incident summary is logged after resolution.
embedding_text:

2. The on-call engineer acknowledges the alert within 10 minutes.
3. The engineer follows the relevant runbook — [incident-high-latency](incident-high-latency.md), [rollback-procedure](rollback-procedure.md), or [rotate-api-keys](rotate-api-keys.md) depending on the alert type.
4. If unresolved within 30 minutes, the engineer escalates to the squad lead for the affected service.
5. A post-incident summary is logged after resolution.

- See [notification-service](notification-service.md) — delivery failures here also trigger this process

text: Standard procedure for rolling back a bad deployment across platform services.
embedding_text: rollback-procedure - Overview: Standard procedure for rolling back a bad deployment across platform services.

text: Platform Team - on-call engineer
embedding_text: rollback-procedure - Owner: Platform Team - on-call engineer

text:
- [orders-api](orders-api.md)
- [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md)
embedding_text:
rollback-procedure - Applies To: - [orders-api](orders-api.md)
- [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md)

text:
1. Identify the last known-good version from the CI/CD deployment history.
2. Trigger a rollback via the deployment pipeline to that version.
3. Confirm the rollback completed by checking the service's health endpoint.
4. Monitor the relevant metric for the affected service (e.g. [p99-latency-orders-api](p99-latency-orders-api.md) or [agent-success-rate](agent-success-rate.md)) for 15 minutes post-rollback.
5. Notify the on-call channel and update the incident log.
embedding_text:
rollback-procedure - Steps: 1. Identify the last known-good version from the CI/CD deployment history.
2. Trigger a rollback via the deployment pipeline to that version.
3. Confirm the rollback completed by checking the service's health endpoint.
4. Monitor the relevant metric for the affected service (e.g. [p99-latency-orders-api](p99-latency-orders-api.md) or [agent-success-rate](agent-success-rate.md)) for 15 minutes post-rollback.
5. Notify the on-call channel and update the incident log.

- See [incident-high-latency](incident-high-latency.md) — an example incident where this procedure was used

text: Procedure for rotating credentials and API keys managed by auth-service.
embedding_text: rotate-api-keys - Overview: Procedure for rotating credentials and API keys managed by auth-service.

text: Platform Team - Identity & Security squad
embedding_text: rotate-api-keys - Owner: Platform Team - Identity & Security squad

text:
1. Generate new credentials via the auth-service admin panel.
2. Distribute new keys to dependent services ([orders-api](orders-api.md), [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md), [notification-service](notification-service.md)) through the secrets manager.
3. Deploy dependent services to pick up the new keys.
4. Revoke the old keys after confirming all services are healthy on the new credentials.
5. Monitor [auth-failure-rate](auth-failure-rate.md) for a temporary spike during the transition window.
embedding_text:
rotate-api-keys - Steps: 1. Generate new credentials via the auth-service admin panel.
2. Distribute new keys to dependent services ([orders-api](orders-api.md), [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md), [notification-service](notification-service.md)) through the secrets manager.
3. Deploy dependent services to pick up the new keys.
4. Revoke the old keys after confirming all services are healthy on the new credentials.
5. Monitor [auth-failure-rate](auth-failure-rate.md) for a temporary spike during the transition window.

text:
- See [auth-service](auth-service.md) for the owning service
- See [on-call-process](on-call-process.md) if failure rate spikes beyond expected levels

text: Procedure for scaling embedding-service horizontally when throughput approaches capacity.
embedding_text: scale-embedding-service - Overview: Procedure for scaling embedding-service horizontally when throughput approaches capacity.

text: Platform Team - ML Infrastructure squad
embedding_text: scale-embedding-service - Owner: Platform Team - ML Infrastructure squad

text:
1. Check [embedding-service-throughput](embedding-service-throughput.md) to confirm the service is near its target ceiling.
2. Increase the replica count for embedding-service in the Kubernetes deployment config.
3. Confirm new replicas pass health checks and are receiving traffic via the load balancer.
4. Re-run the load testing suite to confirm the new throughput ceiling.
5. Update [embedding-service-throughput](embedding-service-throughput.md) with the new baseline once confirmed.
embedding_text:
scale-embedding-service - Steps: 1. Check [embedding-service-throughput](embedding-service-throughput.md) to confirm the service is near its target ceiling.
2. Increase the replica count for embedding-service in the Kubernetes deployment config.
3. Confirm new replicas pass health checks and are receiving traffic via the load balancer.
4. Re-run the load testing suite to confirm the new throughput ceiling.
5. Update [embedding-service-throughput](embedding-service-throughput.md) with the new baseline once confirmed.

text:
- See [embedding-service](embedding-service.md) for the owning service
- See [incident-high-latency](incident-high-latency.md) — a past incident where this procedure was triggered

text: High-level overview of how the AI Platform Team's services fit together.
embedding_text: system-architecture-overview - Overview: High-level overview of how the AI Platform Team's services fit together.

text:
- [auth-service](auth-service.md) — validates identity for every other service
- [orders-api](orders-api.md) — handles the e-commerce checkout write path
- [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md) — manages AI agent lifecycle and task routing

system-architecture-overview - Core Services: - [auth-service](auth-service.md) — validates identity for every other service
- [orders-api](orders-api.md) — handles the e-commerce checkout write path
- [agent-orchestrator](agent-orchestrator.md) — manages AI agent lifecycle and task routing
- [embedding-service](embedding-service.md) — provides vector embeddings for agents and search
- [notification-service](notification-service.md) — sends emails and push notifications

text: A typical request enters through orders-api or agent-orchestrator, is authenticated by auth-service, and may trigger downstream calls to embedding-service (for agent tasks) or notification-service (for order confirmations).
